title: Ensuring Referential Integrity
text: Ensuring Referential Integrity You can ensure the referential integrity of data in Uniface applications by defining constraints in object properties, ProcScript, and database scripts. Uniface provides the following mechanisms for ensuring referential integrity: Table 1. Ensuring referential integrity Constraints How to implement Declarative constraints Create declarative definitions for relationships and foreign key rules in the modeled objects. If both entities in a relationship are correctly handled in the component data structure, Uniface automatically enforces the constraints you have defined for that relationship. Database triggers via the Create Table facility Use the Create Table facility to create database triggers that enforce delete constraints on the table in the database. This means that the constraints are enforced both when a Uniface application deletes a record and when a non-Uniface application deletes a record. Customized constraints Define these constraints using ProcScript in Uniface triggers (for example, the leaveModifiedKey trigger). This is a very specialized way of enforcing constraints in specific circumstances. For more information about whether your driver supports database referential integrity, consult the appropriate DBMS connector documentation. Related Links Referential Integrity Referential integrity is a standard feature of relational database design that prevents users or applications from entering inconsistent data. Most relational databases have referential integrity rules that you can apply when you create a relationship between tables. Database Connectors To access, retrieve, and store data, Uniface provides database connectors that translate Uniface I/O requests into the target database's data definition and manipulation languages.
